name: TCP/IP DAT
description: The TCP/IP DAT is used for sending and receiving information over a TCP/IP connection between two remotely located computers. It captures all the messages without any queuing or buffering, and allows you to send it any messages you want. Send messages using the [[tcpipDAT_Class]]. Handle received messages using the callback DAT attached to the TCP/IP DAT. See [[Network Protocols]]. This DAT can be used to intercept all the raw information being sent from a Pipe Out CHOP for example. The [[Pipe In CHOP]] and [[Pipe Out CHOP]] also communicate through a TCP/IP connection, though they use a specific syntax. See also [[Peer Class]], [[UDP In DAT]], [[UDP Out DAT]]. For Tscript. see Tscript send Command. '''NOTE for Windows OS - If experiencing connection issues make sure Windows Firewall is disabled.'''

parameters.name: Active
parameters.label: Active
parameters.description: This check box enables the connection.

parameters.name: Connection Mode
parameters.label: Connection Mode
parameters.description: Specify if this operator is communicating as a '''client''' or a '''server'''.

parameters.name: Network Address
parameters.label: Network Address
parameters.description: If this node is communicating as a client, this should be the IP address of the server.

parameters.name: Port
parameters.label: Port
parameters.description: The network port to listen on or connect to, depending on of the node is the server or client respectively.

parameters.name: Shared Connection
parameters.label: Shared Connection
parameters.description: Use the same connection as other networking DATs using the same network protocol.

parameters.name: Row/Callback Format
parameters.label: Row/Callback Format
parameters.description: Determines how the incoming data is parsed into the table.

parameters.name: Local Address
parameters.label: Local Address
parameters.description: Specify an IP address to listen on when in server mode, useful when the system has multiple NICs (Network Interface Card) and you want to select which one to use. When left blank, the TCP/IP DAT will listen on all interfaces.

parameters.name: Callbacks DAT
parameters.label: Callbacks DAT
parameters.description: The Callbacks DAT will execute once for each message received.

parameters.name: Execute from
parameters.label: Execute from
parameters.description: Determines the location the script is run from.

parameters.name: From Operator
parameters.label: From Operator
parameters.description: The component who's state change will trigger the DAT to execute its script when Execute from is set to On Panel Change. This component is also the path that the script will be executed from if the Execute from parameter is set to Specified Component.

parameters.name: Clamp Output
parameters.label: Clamp Output
parameters.description: The DAT is limited to 100 messages by default but with Clamp Output, this can be set to anything including unlimited.

parameters.name: Maximum Lines
parameters.label: Maximum Lines
parameters.description: Limits the number of messages, older messages are removed from the list first.

parameters.name: Clear Output
parameters.label: Clear Output
parameters.description: Deletes all lines except the heading. To clear with a script command, here is an example: opparm -c /serial1 clear

parameters.name: Bytes Column
parameters.label: Bytes Column
parameters.description: Outputs the raw bytes of the message in a separate column.
